questions.question: What is the primary function of error detection in data transmission?
options: A) Error correction
B) Data duplication
C) Data validation
D) Error detection
questions.explanation: Error detection is the process of identifying errors that may have occurred during data transmission.

questions.question: What type of code is used for error correction in digital communication systems?
options: A) Cyclic Codes
B) Block Codes
C) Convolutional Codes
D) All of the above
questions.explanation: Block codes, including cyclic codes, are used for error correction in digital communication systems.

questions.question: What is the main difference between cyclic codes and non-cyclic codes?
options: A) Cyclic codes are used for error correction while non-cyclic codes are used for error detection
B) Cyclic codes are used for error detection while non-cyclic codes are used for error correction
C) Cyclic codes are used for data transmission while non-cyclic codes are used for data reception
D) Cyclic codes are used for data transmission while non-cyclic codes are used for data reception
questions.explanation: Cyclic codes are used for error correction, while non-cyclic codes are used for error detection.

questions.question: What is the purpose of a parity bit in a block code?
options: A) To add redundancy to the data
B) To detect errors in the data
C) To encode the data
D) To decode the data
questions.explanation: A parity bit is used to add redundancy to the data, making it easier to detect errors.

questions.question: What is the advantage of using cyclic codes over non-cyclic codes?
options: A) Cyclic codes are slower than non-cyclic codes
B) Cyclic codes are more complex to implement than non-cyclic codes
C) Cyclic codes can detect errors more efficiently than non-cyclic codes
D) Cyclic codes can correct errors more efficiently than non-cyclic codes
questions.explanation: Cyclic codes can correct errors more efficiently than non-cyclic codes.
